english: or rendered impossible Mil.; sgom sruṅ-ba (sgom srung ba) to sustain, to preserve meditation (undisturbed) Mil.; sgom-méd (sgom med) without meditation Thgr.

I. vb., pf. bsgoms (bsgoms), fut. bsgom (bsgom), imp. sgom(s) (sgom(s)), resp. t'ugs sgóm-pa (thugs sgom pa) (Ssk. भू, causative भावय) 1. originally: to fancy, imagine; meditate, contemplate, consider, c. accus. and dat.; to have, to entertain, to produce in one's mind, = skyéd-pa (skyed pa), e. g. bzód-pa (bzod pa), sñiṅ-rje (snying rje), byáms-pa (byams pa) etc.; rgyún-du nam či č'á-med sgom (rgyun du nam ci cha med sgom) always consider that it is uncertain at what time you shall die Mil.; with the accus. and termin., or with a double accus.; to look upon, to represent to one's self as ..., gró-drug-séms-čan p'á-mar sgom ('gro drug sems can pha mar sgom) look upon the beings of the six classes as being your parents Mil., viz. with the same respect and affection, or even so, that you imagine your father's or your mother's soul inhabiting just now the animal body of one of those beings; rmí-lam sgyú-ma sgom (rmi lam sgyu ma sgom) look upon it as being the illusion of a dream Mil. — 2. In later times sgóm-pa (sgom pa) became the usual term for the systematic meditation of the Buddhist saint, so that this word, and the expressions tiṅ-ṅe-_dzín-du _júg-pa (ting nge 'dzin du 'jug pa), and bsam-gtán sgrúb-pa (bsam gtan sgrub pa), which in classical writings denote the concentration of the mind upon one point or subject, e. g. upon a certain deity, lha (lha), prob. imply one and the same thing. Three degrees of this systematic meditation are to be distinguished, viz. ltá-ba (lta ba) contemplation, sgóm-pa (sgom pa) meditation, properly so called, (which requires gsal (gsal) daṅ mi-rtog má-yyeṅs gsum (dang mi rtog ma g.yengs gsum), i.e. that it be performed in a clear and decided manner, without suffering one's self to be disturbed or distracted by any thing), and the third degree spyód-pa (spyod pa), exercise and practice, which three distinctions will be somewhat elucidated by the following: bzá(-baí)-gtad(-so) yód-na bltá-ba min, byíṅ-rgod yód-na sgóm-pa min, bláṅ-dor yód-na spyód-pa min (bza' (-ba'i) -gtad (-so) yod na blta ba min, bying rgod yod na sgom pa min, blang dor yod na spyod pa min), if one lives plenteously, there is no contemplation (possible); where there is inattention and a distracted mind, meditation cannot take place; where there is desire or disgust, exercise and practice are not (to be thought of) Mil. 14, 11. Hence contemplation would seem to be more immediately opposed to the rule of sense, meditation to the rule of imagination, practice to the rule of passion; v. also Was. (137), Köpp. I, 585. Sometimes contemplation and meditation are also opposed to t'ós-pa (thos pa), and bsám-pa (bsam pa), hearing and knowing, as to mere acts of memory and intellect. — sgóm(-pa)-po (sgom (-pa) -po) Cs., sgom-byéd (sgom byed), sgóm-mk'an (sgom mkhan) Mil. one that meditates, an ascetic; sgóm-ma (sgom ma) fem. Mil. — sgom-č'én (sgom chen) 1. a great meditator (so Mil. often calls himself). 2. a kind of field-mouse, Lagomys badius Hook. II, 156. — sgom-t'ág (sgom thag) 'meditating-cord', a cord or rope slung by the laxer sects round their bodies, in order to facilitate the effort of maintaining an erect and immoveable posture during meditation, which expedient of course is scorned by the more rigid devotees. II. sbst. 1. meditation. — 2. Cs.: 'the state of being accustomed to' (prob. erron. for goms-pa (goms pa)).
a spindle in turning-lathes? v. the next word.
1. pf. and fut. bsgar (bsgar), to boil down, to condense by boiling, e. g. bu-rám (bu ram) sugar. — 2. to turn on a lathe, W. *gór-la ten-če'* (*gor la ten che'*).

(perh. also skór-mo (skor mo)) 1. round, e. g. of leaves, Wdn. and elsewh. — 2. a circle. — 3. a disk, a globe; hence a rupee W.; a semi-globular bowl or vessel W., sgor-t'íg (sgor thig) circular line, circumference, circle; sgor-t'íg p'yé-ba (sgor thig phye ba) Cs., p'yéd-ka (phyed ka) Schr. semicircle.

in compounds and as adverb: private, separate, distinct; privately etc., opp. to spyi (spyi), e. g. spyi-gdugs (spyi gdugs) a parasol for several persons, awning, shelter, sgos-gdugs (sgos gdugs) a parasol for one person Glr.; sgós-skal (sgos skal) share of a single person, individual lot
